cv_text:
## <CANDIDATE_NAME>

<PHONE> | <EMAIL> | <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

## PROFESSIONAL STATEMENT

Formally trained engineer with extensive experience in designing and implementing high-throughput screening assays and laboratory automation systems using confocal microscopes, image analysis tools, and scripts (Python, ImageJ, MATLAB).  Proven ability to independently manage complex projects in various fields from concept to validation, with strong proficiency in cell assays, microscopy, and material characterization. Motivated self-starter evidenced by enrollment into data science courses and creation of tools/scripts to streamline processes or tasks.

## EDUCATION

Ph.D Chemical Engineering Candidate, Shoichet Lab, GPA:4.0/4.0

University of Toronto

Sept 2020 - Present

Relevant Coursework:

UofT DSI Data Science Certificate - Database structure and querying using SQLite, version tracking using Git CHE1148H Process Data Analytics - Python and <CANDIDATE_NAME> notebooks, Data handling and analysis using Pandas, machine learning algorithms

B.ASc Nanotechnology Engineering, University of Waterloo GPA:3.8/4.0

Sept 2013 - May 2018

## WORK EXPERIENCE

## Graduate Researcher

The Shoichet Lab (PI: Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME>)

- Develop and modify polymeric systems for the investigation of liver and lung disease models using disease and healthy patient cells.
- Create automated imaging and analysis processes for high throughput imaging systems (<LOCATION>) and confocal microscopy systems for the laboratory.
- Utilize Excel, Python, MATLAB, and ImageJ to clean, analyze, and visualize large amounts of quantitative and qualitative data. Automated processes for time savings of 10 fold at minimum.
- Conduct literature review and keep up to date with literature to identify gaps and trends in fields.

## Fellow

Biomineralization and Biomimetics Lab (PI: Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME>)

Harvard University & Massachusetts Eye and Ear

- Developed  microbial  and  cellular  adhesion  assays  on  omniphobic  materials  for  tympanostomy  tubes showing a reduction of up to 99% of bacterial attachment.
- Interfaced  with  the  animal  study  and  development  team  to  provide  analysis,  feedback,  and  potential improvements for in vivo studies.

## Hardware Engineering Intern

Verily (formerly Google Life Sciences)

- Spearheaded  the  formulation  and  characterization  of  a  novel  polymer  platform  for  implantable  devices resulting in 7 different formulations.
- Initiated biocompatibility tests in compliance with ISO 10993.
- Interfaced with various teams to develop new in-house procedures and testing protocols.

May 2017 -Aug 2017

Oct 2018 -Oct 2019

July 2020 -Present

## Research Assistant

Biomechanics and Cell Mechanics Laboratory (PI: Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME>) Soochow University

- Developed, synthesized, and conducted experiments on novel polymers for tissue engineering applications.
- Designed, prototyped, and tested multiple versions of knee braces for genu <CANDIDATE_NAME> (knock- knee) treatment using CAD and 3D printing.
- Wrote technical documents on completed work resulting in three published papers.

## Research Assistant

Micro Engineering Dynamics and Automation Lab (PI: Dr. <CANDIDATE_NAME>) University of Calgary

- Revised and extensively tested a novel aluminum-air battery. Led to a 24-fold increase in performance.
- Assembled enclosures and holders for battery testing using CAD, 3D printing, and machining.
- Designed and built a small-scale roller system and environmental controllers using Arduino.
- Completed work set the foundation for one publication.

## INVOLVEMENT

## Co-chair and Advisor

Toronto Biomedical Engineering Conference

- Oversee conference planning duties such as inviting speakers, developing workshops, managing a team of 11 people, as well as booking venues and catering for the event. Increased attendance by 66.8% from 2022-2023

## Business Lead and Technical Advisor

University of Waterloo Nanorobotics Group (UW\_NRG)

- Designed, tested, and debugged multiple microrobot setups as well as their control systems.
- Constructed stages for the microrobots using machining and 3D printing.
- Wrote and presented proposals to potential sponsors. Secured over $20,000 in funds for UW\_NRG.
- Competed in the annual Mobile Microrobotics Challenge at the International Conference on Robotics and Automation. Two 1 st  place finishes (2013, 2016), one 2 nd  place finish (2015), one 3 rd  place finish (2014).

## SIDEPROJECT

Japan Top Restaurants Map and <CANDIDATE_NAME> to scrape over 5000 restaurant entries from a Japanese food review website for the top restaurants in multiple cities. Organized, filtered, and displayed the restaurants on Google Sheets for offline access/planning as well as Google maps for quick navigation. The tool was released publicly on <LOCATION> and garnered over 48,000 views and 1,700 shares within a day. https://github.com/cwfling/Top-Japanese-Restaurants-Tabelog

## SKILLS

Programming:

Python, MATLAB, ImageJ, SQL , Java

Software:

Microsoft Office, GraphPad, Google Suite (Docs, Sheets, Slides), Adobe Photoshop/Illustrator,

Solidworks

Languages:

English, Chinese (Hakka dialect)

2013 -2018

Sep 2015 -Apr 2016

Jan 2015 -Apr 2015

September 2022-Present
pitch_text:
Selected Job Offer Position: Data Scientist, Drug Discovery (Hypothetical Biotech Company) Key Requirements:

- PhD in Engineering, Biology, or related field.
- Proficiency in <CANDIDATE_NAME> (Pandas, <LOCATION>-learn) and SQL.
- Experience with high-throughput screening data and image analysis.
- Ability to translate biological questions into data problems.

## The Elevator Pitch

"Hi, I'm <CANDIDATE_NAME>. I'm a PhD candidate in Chemical Engineering at U of T, but I think of myself as an engineer who speaks fluent 'data.'

In my research, I realized that the biggest bottleneck wasn't the science itself, but how we handled the data. I didn't just run experiments; I built automated Python pipelines and imaging systems that reduced our analysis time by 10-fold. I essentially turned our wet lab into a data factory.

I noticed you're looking for someone to bridge the gap between high-throughput screening and data science. Because I've actually done the pipetting and the coding-from analyzing confocal microscopy images to scraping 5,000 datasets for my own side projects-I can clean and interpret your biological data faster and with more context than a standard data scientist.

I'm looking to bring that efficiency to a team like yours where I can help accelerate the discovery pipeline. I'd love to hear how your team is currently handling its screening data."

<LOCATION>

clarify_text.ikigai:
My Ikigai centers on accelerating innovation through automation and data intelligence .

The intersection of my passions and skills lies in identifying bottlenecks in complex scientific processes and removing them. I love the "detective work" of analyzing a problem (whether in batteries, tissue engineering, or metallurgy) and the satisfaction of building a tool that solves it permanently. My talents in Python, microscopy, and hardware engineering allow me to bridge the gap between traditional wet-lab science and modern data science-a gap that the world (specifically the biotech and medical technology sectors) desperately needs to close to improve healthcare outcomes.

I am driven to create a lasting impact by building systems that allow scientists to focus on discovery rather than repetitive tasks. To align my career with this, I am pivoting from pure academic research to industry roles where scalability is key, such as Data Science or Automation Engineering. I am ready to compromise on the "freedom" of academic exploration to achieve the tangible, high-velocity impact of industry product development. Living my Ikigai means constantly learning new tools (like my recent deep dive into SQL and Machine Learning) to ensure I am always the most efficient problem-solver in the room.
clarify_text.ideal_day:
My ideal professional day is a mix of technical problem-solving and cross-functional collaboration. It starts with a brief team stand-up where we align on the day's sprint-I thrive in environments where engineering and science teams interface, like I did at Verily.

The morning is deep-work time: I am coding a Python script to automate a data pipeline or refining an image analysis algorithm. I enjoy the "flow state" of debugging and optimizing code to shave seconds or minutes off a process. <CANDIDATE_NAME> is social; I enjoy connecting with colleagues from different backgrounds to learn about their specific challenges.

In the afternoon, I switch to "bridge" mode-translating complex data insights into actionable summaries for stakeholders, similar to how I managed the Biomedical Engineering Conference. I might spend an hour testing a new hardware prototype or validating a model against real-world data. The day ends with a sense of tangible progress-having built a tool or generated an insight that didn't exist in the morning.
clarify_text.smart_goals:
- Goal 1 (Career): Secure a full-time position as a Data Scientist or Automation Engineer in the Greater Toronto Area or remotely by May 2026.
- Measurable: Apply to 5 relevant jobs per week and secure at least 3 interviews per month.
- Attainable: leveraging my PhD defense timeline and current interview momentum.
- Goal 2 (Technical Skill): Complete the UofT DSI Data Science Certificate and build one end-to-end MLOps project (deploying a model to the cloud) by April 1, 2026.
- Measurable: Certificate issued; GitHub repository live with deployment documentation.
- Realistic: I am already enrolled and have a foundation in Python.
- Goal 3 (Networking): Expand my professional network outside of academia by connecting with 20 industry professionals on LinkedIn in the HealthTech space by June 2026.
- Measurable: Send 5 personalized connection requests weekly; schedule 1 coffee chat per month.
- Time-bound: Start immediately, review progress monthly.

preferences.recent_interviews: Yes for automation process engineer roles
